name: h264-profile-level-id

metadata.name: h264-profile-level-id
metadata.summary: Python utility to process H264 profile-level-id values based on Google's libwebrtc C++ code.

documentation:
# h264-profile-level-id

Python utility to process [H264](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6184) `profile-level-id` values based on Google's [libwebrtc](https://chromium.googlesource.com/external/webrtc/+/refs/heads/master/media/base/h264_profile_level_id.h) C++ code.

## Reference
[JavaScript Version h264-profile-level-id](https://github.com/ibc/h264-profile-level-id)

modules.name: h264_profile_level_id.core
documentation: h264-profile-level-id
Profile

functions.name: h264_profile_level_id.core.byteMaskString
documentation: Convert a string of 8 characters into a byte where the positions containing
character c will have their bit set. For example, c = 'x', str = "x1xx0000"
will return 0b10110000.

functions.name: h264_profile_level_id.core.parseProfileLevelId
documentation: Parse profile level id that is represented as a string of 3 hex bytes.
Nothing will be returned if the string is not a recognized H264 profile
level id.
@param {String} str - profile-level-id value as a string of 3 hex bytes.
@returns {ProfileLevelId}

functions.name: h264_profile_level_id.core.profileLevelIdToString
documentation: Returns canonical string representation as three hex bytes of the profile
level id, or returns nothing for invalid profile level ids.
@param {ProfileLevelId} profile_level_id
@returns {String}

functions.name: h264_profile_level_id.core.parseSdpProfileLevelId
documentation: Parse profile level id that is represented as a string of 3 hex bytes
contained in an SDP key-value map. A default profile level id will be
returned if the profile-level-id key is missing. Nothing will be returned if
the key is present but the string is invalid.
@param {Object} [params={}] - Codec parameters object.
@returns {ProfileLevelId}

functions.name: h264_profile_level_id.core.isSameProfile
documentation: Returns True if the parameters have the same H264 profile, i.e. the same
H264 profile (Baseline, High, etc).
@param {Object} [params1={}] - Codec parameters object.
@param {Object} [params2={}] - Codec parameters object.
@returns {Boolean}

functions.name: h264_profile_level_id.core.generateProfileLevelIdForAnswer
documentation: Generate codec parameters that will be used as answer in an SDP negotiation
based on local supported parameters and remote offered parameters. Both
local_supported_params and remote_offered_params represent sendrecv media
descriptions, i.e they are a mix of both encode and decode capabilities. In
theory, when the profile in local_supported_params represent a strict superset
of the profile in remote_offered_params, we could limit the profile in the
answer to the profile in remote_offered_params.
However, to simplify the code, each supported H264 profile should be listed
explicitly in the list of local supported codecs, even if they are redundant.
Then each local codec in the list should be tested one at a time against the
remote codec, and only when the profiles are equal should this function be
called. Therefore, this function does not need to handle profile intersection,
and the profile of local_supported_params and remote_offered_params must be
equal before calling this function. The parameters that are used when
negotiating are the level part of profile-level-id and level-asymmetry-allowed.
@param {Object} [local_supported_params={}]
@param {Object} [remote_offered_params={}]
@returns {String} Canonical string representation as three hex bytes of the
profile level id, or null if no one of the params have profile-level-id.
@throws {TypeError} If Profile mismatch or invalid params.

functions.name: h264_profile_level_id.core.isLessLevel
documentation: Compare H264 levels and handle the level 1b case.

classes.name: h264_profile_level_id.core.BitPattern
documentation: Class for matching bit patterns such as "x1xx0000" where 'x' is allowed to be
either 0 or 1.
